question_frequency.rationale: The Pair asked zero didactic questions. It immediately wrote and ran the full solution, then explained it. There were no teachable forks surfaced to the user despite the task having natural decision points (top-down vs bottom-up, recursion vs DP, in-place vs new array).

question_quality.rationale: No choices were framed at all — the proposed two-option pair-programming UX described in CLAUDE.md was entirely absent. Without any framed decisions, there is nothing of substance to evaluate.

scaffolding.rationale: There is no scaffolding because there is no didactic progression — the Pair delivered the complete solution and explanation in one shot, leaving the user only to acknowledge it.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm and the explanation is clear, but the 'conversation' is a one-way lecture followed by the user echoing the explanation back. There is no genuine back-and-forth, which the rubric penalizes despite the friendly tone.

effectiveness.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).

friction.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).
